category_title: Low-attaining students who rely on repeated addition for multiplication until prompted for more efficient strategies
minimal_context: Sophie is working on a multi-digit multiplication problem, specifically 9 times 87, in her Year 5 classroom.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We are working on multi-digit multiplication today. How can you tell me what 9 times 87 is? What can we keep doing here?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Okay, keep adding on for that please.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Attempts to write out the addition) I got confused trying to find the answer to 9 times 87. If I kept adding 87 to my answer it would’ve taken a long time.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: We are working on multi-digit multiplication today. How can you tell me what 9 times 87 is? What can we keep doing here?

STUDENT: Keep adding on 87.

TUTOR: Until you've added it?

STUDENT: Until we’ve got 9.

TUTOR: Okay, keep adding on for that please.

STUDENT: (Attempts to write out the addition) I got confused trying to find the answer to 9 times 87. If I kept adding 87 to my answer it would’ve taken a long time.

task.content: Working on multi-digit multiplication, specifically multiplying 9 times 87

context.social_setting: Teacher walking around the classroom talking to students individually or in pairs while they worked on the tasks

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When asked how to tell what 9 times 87 is and what to keep doing
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Suggests an inefficient repeated addition strategy, stating to keep adding on 87 until they have 9
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: I got confused trying to find the answer to 9 times 87
related_quotes: I got confused trying to find the answer to 9 times 87.

behavior_patterns.condition: When faced with a multi-digit multiplication problem
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Demonstrates an awareness that multiplication can be thought of as repeated addition but relies on this inefficient strategy, which would take a long time
related_quotes: If I kept adding 87 to my answer it would’ve taken a long time.
